text:
We've wrapped on Day 12 of the 2024 Paris Olympics and the action didn't disappoint.

There were more Team GB medal hopes in action. Sailor Micky Beckett started the day in fourth place ahead of the medal race in the men's dinghy category, and John Gimson and Anna Burnet went in the mixed multihull gold medal race.

In the track cycling men's team pursuit final, the quartet of Ethan Hayter, Ollie Wood, Ethan Vernon and Charlie Tanfield took on Australia, while the track and field action continued. There has also been further boxing fallout, after Imane Khelif defied her critics to make the women's 66kg final.

You can recap all the latest Olympics news in our LIVE blog, below....

Sunday's closing ceremony is fast approaching, with only a few more days of Olympic action to come.

Thank you for following our coverage from Day 12 of the game.

Same again tomorrow?

Coco Gauffreveals she previously 'didn't have the confidence' to approachAndy Murrayfor a photo.

Speaking to Puntodebreak.com about her chance meeting with the Team GB Olympian, Gauff said: “I finally took a photo withRafa Nadal, I didn't post the photo, but I probably should have… I also finally took a photo with Andy Murray, which I had always wanted to ask him for.

“So I managed to accomplish my two goals, getting a photo with the two of them. Ironically they are tennis players, so I could have gotten them in any tournament, but I don't know, I just felt like I didn't have the confidence.”

\u201cI could sit here for hours talking about all the cool people I met (Olympics). I finally took a photo with Rafa Nadal, I didn't post the photo, but I probably should have\u2026 I also finally\u2026pic.twitter.com/F6xWbVM6HY

Matthew Hudson-Smith put on a brave face as he reacted to missing out on gold in the men's 400m by a matter of inches.

He told theBBC: "Yeah it's crazy. Honestly, sometimes the journey is better than the outcome and it's been a hell of a journey.

"Came away with an Olympic silver, how many people in the world can say that. I've been the bridesmaid twice now, my time is coming. I'm just happy.

"I saw my family, I didn't even know they were here. It was a bit of a shock really because I hate them watching me."

Over in the men's 400m final, Matthew Hudson-Smith came within a couple of inches of winning gold.

Hudson-Smith ran a remarkable race, completing one lap of the track in under 44.50 seconds.

However, it wasn't enough, with Quincy Hall pipping him to gold on the line by less than 0.20 seconds.

READ MORE

Amber Anning booked her spot in the women's 400m final with a stellar 49.47 race.

Having the season of her life, Anning will now compete for a medal in one of the showpiece events of the Games.

In the previous semi-final, Laviai Nielsen narrowly missed out on progression to the 400m final by less than one second.

The Team GB athlete finished third in her semi-final, 0.69 seconds behind second-placed Alexis Holmes.

Earlier in the evening Victoria Ohuruogu also missed out on progression, finishing fifth in her semi-final.

Noah Lyles missed out on first place in his 200m semi-final.

Letsile Tebogowon won the semi-final with a time of 19.96 seconds.

Lyles did progress in second but did so posting a time over 20 seconds.

As a result, his lane for the final will likely be impacted.

Lyles has previously stated that only three golds (100m, 200m and 4x100m) at these Games will suffice.



The medals keep flowing at the velodrome in Paris, with Team GB women taking bronze in the team pursuit.

They were trailing Italy for most of the race, but a valiant effort in the final kilometre saw them claw back the deficit.

Team GB's search for gold in the men's team pursuit ended in heartbreak after a slip-up from Ethan Hayter in the final lap opened the door for Australia.

The quartet can still celebrate a medal, with Team GB taking home a silver medal.



Andy Macdonald earned a rapturous reception from skateboarding fans at the Paris Games.

Unfortunately though, Macdonald - who even brought Tony Hawk to his feet - didn't earn a spot in the next round.

Macdonald finished 18th in the preliminary round of the men's skateboarding.

A result that sparked boos from the Macdonald-loving crowd.

READ MORE

Micky Beckett narrowly missed out on a medal in the men's dinghy race down in Marseille.

The British sailor had solid hopes of bronze, but was served up a penalty turn late into the race.

He ultimately finished sixth, with gold going to Australian Matt Wearn.

The noise has ramped up Le Golf National, and that's because Celine Boutier is now leading on -4.

Awful day so far for Team GB though. Gerogia Hall is +3 on the back nine, and Charley Hull finished +9 after an 81.



How cruel is that.

Micky Beckett was well on the way to silver in the men's dinghy medal race, when organsiers abandoned the race due to a lack of wind.

The frustration continues for Beckett and co. The final was already delayed from yesterday.

Elinor Barker, Josie Knight, Anna Morris and Jess Roberts have set a new national record in their semi-final of the women's team pursuit, but it's not been enough to beat Team USA.

The Team GB quartet still have a bronze medal race with Italy to come.

Emma Finucane has won her heat in the women's keirin first round.

She beats Germany's Lea Friedrich to the win.

Already with one gold medal to her name, the 21-year-old is strongly tipped to win a second one in this event.



Team GB's Charley Hull has now dropped to seven-over par after just 12 holes, leaving her last out of 60 competitors.

Georgia Hall is going better, one-under after three. That's one behind leader Aditi Ashok, of India.

Australian hockey player Tom Craig has reportedly been arrested on suspicion of trying to buy cocaine in Paris.

Police arrested two men in the middle of a transaction, according toFranceinfo, with Craig said to be held on suspicion of possessing one gram of cocaine.

In a statement, the Australian Olympic Committee confirmed: "An Australian hockey team member is in custody after being arrested in Paris on August 6th. No charges have been laid. The AOC is continuing to make enquiries and arrange support for the team member."

READ MORE HERE.

Chaos in Paris. After the falls of the first 5,000m heat, the second is nearly ruined by a cameraman wandering across the track.

But now George Mills has been reinstated! The Team GB athlete is still in the Olympics. Wow.

READ MORE HERE.

Chaos in the first 5000m heat!

Team GB's George Mills is one of four athletes to collide and fall in the finishing straight.

Mills was furious afterwards, gesturing at Hugo Hay of France at the finish line. Narve Gilje Nordas of Norway was the winner but expect some result protests.



FormerTeam GBswimmer Sharron Davies wants 'once in a lifetime' sex tests for all female athletes amid the ongoing gender debate at the ParisOlympics.

She hit out again on social media after Imane Khelif made the women's 66kg boxing final.

Please ask WHY won\u2019t the IOC do simple once in a lifetime cheek swab sex tests, when the result would be fair & safe sport for females?

Indian wrestler Inesh Phogat was taken to hospital after being disqualified from the2024 Paris Olympicsfor failing to make weight for her women’s 50kg gold medal match.

She was due to face Team USA’s Sarah Hildebrandt in the final on Thursday night, but the bout was scrapped following her second mandatory weigh-in.

READ MORE HERE.

Maria Perez has seen Spain home to victory in the marathon race walk relay race.

Perez clocked 45:51 as the Spaniards finished 51 seconds clear of Ecuador. Australia narrowly took bronze.

Not a good start for Charley Hull.

On the first at Le Golf National she has racked up a double bogey six. The Team GB medal hope has at least steadied the ship with a par on the second though.



It's Ecuador who are now in the lead after China were hit by penalties. Men's gold medallist Brian Pintado is leading the way but Spain's Alvaro Martin is right behind him.

Italian Massimo Stano is almost 30 seconds back in third.

Paris 2024 organisers and World Aquatics have confirmed that Wednesday's familiarisation session for the marathon swimming in the River Seine will go ahead.

The session was initially scheduled for Tuesday, August 6 but was cancelled because of levels of E. coli.

The women's marathon swimming takes place tomorrow at 6:30am and the men's at the same time on Friday.

Imane Khelif said she “doesn’t care” about the controversy oafter making the final of the women’s 66kg boxing category.

After a a unanimous points win over Janjaem Suwannapheng of Thailand, she said: “I don’t care what anyone is saying about me with the controversy.

“All that is important to me is that I stay on the level and give my people the performance they deserve. I know I’m a talented person and this is a gift to all Algerians.”

We use your sign-up to provide content in ways you've consented to and to improve our understanding of you. This may include adverts from us and 3rd parties based on our understanding. You can unsubscribe at any time. Read ourPrivacy Policy

Team GB golfer Charley Hull is unhappy about not being allowed to smoke on the Le Golf National course today.

Hull and Georgia Hall will both tee off for their first rounds, and the star said: “I do smoke on the course. It’s a habit but I won’t do this week. I don’t think you’re allowed.”

Pressed on if the ban will affect her, she added: “I think it will. Because it relaxes me a little bit.”

Welcome back to our Olympic coverage and the action on day 12 is already underway!

The marathon mixed race walk got underway at 6.30am, with China currently in the lead.

title: Olympics News: Team GB medal hope hits out at ban as Khelif taunts critics | Other | Sport | Express.co.uk
author: Fraser Watson, Amos Murphy
description: Express Sport recaps all the latest Paris Olympics news from Day 12 of the Games.
